title: lobelia tomentosa var. ceratophylla
intro: Lobelia tomentosa var. ceratophylla is a subspecies of the Lobelia tomentosa plant, commonly known as the Indian tobacco or prickly lobelia, which is native to North America and belongs to the Campanulaceae family. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, and has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The health benefits of Lobelia tomentosa var. ceratophylla include its ability to ease bronchial spasms, reduce mucus production, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
